body.u: {Omitted plan of his life which is crossed out but clear to read}

I left school at the age of sixteen, in July of nineteen seventy eight. I had been offered an apprenticeship in Chatom dockyard as a Naval Architect. I enjoyed my four years of training, quite a lot. At first, from leaving school, I {mist?} the twelve weeks of holiday we had compared to only three each year. During my apprenticeship I gained O.N.C, H.N.C. and O.N.D certificates so I finished with quite good qualifications. I stayed on at the dockyard for another three years and became a professional and Technological officer grade two.
During my best year in the dock yard and had got married and had bought a house in Cornwall near where I my next job was. It was a boat building firm and I was one of the directersdirectors.
When I was thirty we started a family and stayed in Cornwall because I had a very secure job, and I kept it until I retired.

I had a very active life, and I enjoyed it very much. When I was middle aged I sailed a great deal in fast catamarans, but as I got older I could move fast enough for them, so I got a cruiser in which I spend most of my free time now.

Looking back I am glad that my life turned out to what it was. Now, I like to watch my family grow up to meeting and facing problems as they go along and sorting them out. I hope that they eventually look back on their life the same way as I do.
